Claim: India is fastest-growing major economy; real GDP ~6.4-7.6% FY24-26
Audit note: IBEF is an industry body citing MoSPI/Economic Survey; the headline growth figure is uncontroversial and cross-confirmed by RBI. Not re-fetched to raw MoSPI table this session.

Claim: Real rural wages grew ~7%/yr (2010-16) then collapsed to ~0-1%/yr
Primary source: Das & Dreze, Labour Bureau 'Wage Rates in Rural India'
Audit note: Used via Countercurrents secondary. The Labour Bureau series is real & public; the Das-Dreze computation should be traced to their original paper/column before terminal citation. Directionally consistent across multiple economists.

Claim: Corporate profit +22.3% vs employment +1.5% (FY24); profit-to-GDP 4.8% (Nifty 500), highest since FY08
Audit note: SBI Research has analytical credibility but is not independent-academic. Reported via The Federal. Trace to the SBI report itself; treat as 'per SBI Research' not fact.

Claim: K-shaped recovery; widening income/wealth inequality
Audit note: CEDA and WIR are credible independent sources. Framing claim, not a single disputed number.

Claim: Jobless growth predates 2014; employment elasticity falling since 1991 liberalisation
Audit note: Well-established in the literature; used as an honesty caveat, not a headline stat.

Claim: 23.4% of Class 3 can read a Class 2 text (ASER 2024)
Audit note: *** MATERIAL CORRECTION *** Primary PDF states 23.4% is GOVERNMENT-SCHOOL Std III specifically, NOT all children. All-India (all schools) Std III reading recovered to ~27.1% (Ideas for India). Pieces present 23.4% as the all-children national figure. MUST FIX.

Claim: 33.7% of Class 3 can do subtraction; 44.8% Class 5 read; 30.7% Class 5 divide
Audit note: 33.7% subtraction is ALL-INDIA (confirmed in PDF). 44.8% Class-5 reading and 30.7% Class-5 division are GOVERNMENT-SCHOOL figures per PDF. Mixed all-India vs govt-school; must label each consistently.

Claim: '3 in 4 Class 3 kids can't read grade-2 text'
Audit note: 76.6% is the inverse of the 23.4% GOVERNMENT-SCHOOL figure. As an all-children claim it overstates: all-India inverse is ~73%. Rhetorically close but technically tied to the govt-school number.

Claim: ASER 2024 = biggest improvement in 20-year history; govt-school led; NEP/FLN driven
Audit note: Confirmed in primary PDF: reading & arithmetic at highest levels since survey inception, driven by govt schools; >80% schools got FLN directive. Honest positive caveat stands.

Claim: NAS (govt survey) statistically unreliable even for state comparisons
Audit note: Peer-reviewed; confirmed in earlier fetch. Solid.

Claim: 80% of Indian engineering grads unemployable
Audit note: Aspiring Minds is a commercial testing firm; figure widely cited but has commercial incentive and methodology is proprietary. Use as 'per Aspiring Minds', pair with India Skills Report for balance.

Claim: 83% of 2024 eng grads without job/internship; 71.5% rated job-ready
Primary source: Unstop Talent Report 2025 / India Skills Report 2025
Audit note: Both are private industry surveys (Unstop; Wheebox/AICTE consortium). Credible-ish but not academic. The 83%-vs-71.5% juxtaposition is the strongest single 'credential vs capability' data point; attribute clearly.

Claim: <7% of male grads got a permanent salaried job within a year (2023)
Primary source: State of Working India 2026, Azim Premji University
Audit note: Azim Premji Univ / CSE is a strong independent academic source. Figure reported via UPSC-prep secondary; trace to the SWI 2026 report PDF to confirm exact wording/year.

Claim: 62% of top-100 JEE scorers migrate; 36% of top-1000; 9/10 of top-10
Primary source: Ganguli et al., J. of Development Economics / NBER
Audit note: Confirmed against ScienceDirect abstract in prior research. Strongest single source in the whole essay. Peer-reviewed, methodology public.

Claim: ~1/3 of new MBBS physicians emigrate annually
Primary source: MEA Lok Sabha replies / Deccan Herald 'Stop the Talent Drain'
Audit note: The migration estimate is an 'estimate' per the op-ed, not a hard govt count. Soften to '~one-third (estimated)'.

Claim: India = 3.12M highly-educated migrants in OECD, largest of any country
Audit note: OECD IMD is authoritative and downloadable; figure cited via Deccan Herald summarising OECD. Trace to OECD table to confirm the 3.12M vs China 2.25M comparison and year (2015/16 vintage).

Claim: Student outflow 7.5 lakh (2022) -> 13.3 lakh (2024)
Audit note: Attributed to EAM's Lok Sabha reply; strong provenance. Cross-confirmed by Deccan Herald and World Bank blog.

Claim: ~5,100 millionaires left 2023; ~3,500 projected 2025; ~$26B
Audit note: Henley report is paywalled/proprietary; headline figures are widely quoted but methodology (uses LinkedIn + wealth intel) is contested. Numbers vary by outlet. Present as 'per Henley' and note falling trend + rising UHNW base (Knight Frank) for balance.

Claim: Stanford 2025 AI Vibrancy: US 78.6, China 36.95, India 21.59
Audit note: Stanford HAI AI Index is authoritative; specific scores cited via Business Today. Trace to the Vibrancy Tool to confirm exact scores (they update). India ranking '3rd' is corroborated multiple places.

Claim: India = #1 origin of immigrant US-unicorn founders: 96 companies
Audit note: NFAP report widely reported June 2026; 96 (India) > Israel 60 > UK 47 > China 41 consistent across Business Standard, Telangana Today, India Weekly. Solid.

Claim: 60% of top US AI firms have >=1 immigrant founder; India leads with 9 founders; 70%+ arrived on student visas
Audit note: IFP analysis of Forbes AI 50 explicitly states India leads with 9 founders, China 8; 70%+ student-visa origin from CSET 2020. Confirmed in prior fetch.

Claim: India per-capita unicorn productivity low (~2.5 per 100k vs Israel 43)
Audit note: Honest counter-caveat; attributed to Stanford VC Initiative via Crunchbase. Keeps the section fair.

Claim: IndiaAI compute ~38,000 GPUs mid-2025; target 100k by end-2026; near-zero before 2024
Audit note: GPU counts sourced to state media and secondary; numbers shift with each tender. Directionally right (near-zero->tens of thousands). Attribute to IndiaAI Mission.

Claim: GERD stuck ~0.64% of GDP; fell from ~0.90% in 2008
Audit note: 0.64% confirmed across Economic Survey coverage (Forbes India, Business Today, Asia Times). Decline from ~0.90% (2008) per SabrangIndia/arXiv 2411.15045. Strong.

Claim: Private sector = ~36% of R&D vs 75-79% in US/China/Korea
Audit note: Confirmed multiple outlets citing the Survey. Solid.

Claim: 262 researchers/million (India) vs 8,714 (S.Korea); ~40k PhDs/yr; ~85k researchers abroad
Primary source: Asia Times 'India's R&D crisis isn't a money problem'
Audit note: Researcher-density figures ultimately derive from UNESCO/World Bank; cited here via Asia Times. Trace to UNESCO UIS for terminal citation. Directionally uncontroversial.

Claim: CEA cited Lazonick: Indian corporates did buybacks/dividends before building R&D depth ('premature financialisation')
Audit note: Attributed analytical framing; confirmed via Plutus IAS coverage of the CEA's remarks. Fair.

Claim: RDI fund: only ~15% of prior-year allocation utilised
Audit note: Utilisation figure (Rs 3,000cr of Rs 20,000cr) reported via Plutus IAS citing Budget 2026-27. Trace to the budget document / PIB. Strong 'punctures the press release' point if confirmed.

Claim: Electoral bonds struck down as unconstitutional, 15 Feb 2024, unanimous 5-judge bench (Art 19(1)(a))
Primary source: ADR v. Union of India, Supreme Court, 15 Feb 2024
Audit note: Judgment is public and widely confirmed (Stimson, ISAS, Time, Tribune, Nature). Unimpeachable.

Claim: Bonds were ~56% of all political funding at peak
Audit note: 56% figure is Stimson's characterisation. Reasonable but attribute; not a court finding.

Claim: BJP got ~48% of all bond donations; ~90% of CORPORATE bond donations; scheme ~Rs 16,518 cr encashed
Audit note: 48% (Rs 6,566cr of Rs 13,558cr across 20 parties) = ECI data via Tribune. Rs 16,518cr total encashed = Deccan Herald/SC. The '~90% of CORPORATE donations' is an ADR *analysis* (Time cites 'as of last November'), a narrower subset - keep the word CORPORATE, attribute to ADR.

Claim: Court warned scheme could enable 'quid pro quo'
Audit note: Direct from the judgment; confirmed The National / Nature coverage.

Claim: ED registered 193 cases against politicians in 10 yrs; only 2 convictions
Primary source: Ministry of Finance, Rajya Sabha reply, March 2025
Audit note: Confirmed verbatim in Outlook's report of the MoF Parliament reply. Both convictions were Jharkhand ex-ministers. Self-documenting govt data.

Claim: <5% PMLA conviction rate since 2019 (42 of 911)
Primary source: Govt reply to Randeep Surjewala; The Wire
Audit note: 42 of 911 (4.6%) Jan 2019-Oct 2024, MoS Finance reply. Strong.

Claim: ~95% of ED/CBI cases target opposition (up from ~54% pre-2014)
Primary source: Indian Express investigation (2022) + 14-party SC plea + opposition claims
Audit note: MoF explicitly told Parliament it does NOT track cases by party/ruling-vs-opposition (confirmed in Outlook primary fetch). The 95% is from the Indian Express investigation and opposition leaders (Bora/Sule), NOT a govt admission. Pieces imply it's govt-sourced alongside the 193/2. MUST separate provenance.
Action needed: SEPARATE: attribute 95% to Indian Express/opposition, NOT govt

Claim: V-Dem reclassified India 'electoral autocracy' (by 2020); ~20% fall in judicial autonomy 2014-24
Audit note: V-Dem classification widely confirmed. The specific '20% judicial autonomy decline' cited via secondary (indianrepublic.in) - trace to V-Dem dataset for the exact figure.

Claim: Freedom House: 'Free'->'Partly Free' (2021), score 66/100
Primary source: Freedom House, Freedom in the World
Audit note: Widely confirmed; score 66 stable since 2021.

Claim: RSF press-freedom rank 150 -> 161
Audit note: Rank figures confirmed via multiple secondary; RSF publishes annually - note the exact years (150 was ~2020-22 era, 161 more recent). Confirm current-year rank before publishing.

Claim: Emergency (1975-77) was a more severe, formal suspension of liberties; sedition abused across govts
Audit note: Honest historical caveat you specifically requested. Uncontroversial.

Claim: Legal right to dissent survives on paper; practical dissent free of harassment largely gone
Primary source: Journal of Democracy, 'Why India's Democracy Is Dying' (2024)
Audit note: Direct paraphrase of the JoD article's thesis. Strong scholarly framing.

Claim: UPI world-leading real-time payment volume
Audit note: UPI's global-leading real-time volume is well-established (NPCI/BIS). Trace to NPCI monthly stats for a specific number if quantifying.

Claim: BHASHINI: hundreds of millions of inferences/month across 22 languages
Audit note: Inference-volume figure cited via a Medium essay; trace to an official BHASHINI/MeitY source before quantifying. Existence & multilingual scope are solid.

Claim: India = 3rd-largest producer of scholarly publications (up from 7th in 2010)
Audit note: Confirmed across Economic Survey coverage and Deccan Herald. Solid - and the honest R&D caveat prevents overselling it.
